message:
perf: single-pass HTTP/1 header parsing and raptorq decoder retry snapshot/restore

http/h1/codec: Replaced the two-pass design where decode_head() first collected
all headers into a Vec then called body_kind() to re-scan for Transfer-Encoding
and Content-Length. Now tracks transfer_encoding_idx and content_length_idx
inline during the header parsing loop, catching duplicates immediately and
determining body kind from the indices without a second pass. The standalone
body_kind() function is removed. Semantics are identical (RFC 7230 §3.3.3
compliance preserved), but the hot path touches each header exactly once.

raptorq/decoder: Eliminated redundant full matrix clones on every Gaussian
elimination retry. Previously the retry loop cloned base_a (sparse coefficient
matrix) and base_b (dense RHS byte vectors) at the top of each iteration.
Replaced with a lazy retry_snapshot that captures the state once at the decision
point and uses clone_from + a flat-buffer snapshot/restore for the dense RHS,
avoiding allocation churn. Also:
- Added fast-path in verify_decoded_output for trivial source symbols (single
  column, coefficient == 1) that skips the reconstruct-then-compare loop
- Extracted first_mismatch_byte() helper to deduplicate byte comparison logic
- Added snapshot_dense_rhs / restore_dense_rhs helpers for flat-buffer packing
- pivot_row is now allocated once and .fill()'d on retry instead of reallocated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
